goal: Replace the ad-hoc driver at scripts/bootstrap.ts with an Effect-based LoopRunner service plus a schema-validated FeatureContract service. Closes the five limitations listed in bootstrap.ts L11-L15 and the two RunTask.ts L129/L130 TODOs blocking judged-retry. Ships as one vertical slice: FeatureContract + RunTask-as-Tag + LoopRunner + two helpers (gitStatus, archive) + bootstrap.ts shrunk to a 15-line shim.
constraints: Services are Context.Tag subclasses with Live and (where useful) Fake layers. Error channels are tagged unions with _tag discriminators and lowercase-verb constructor helpers (matches filesystemError / spawnFailed pattern already in src/services/AgentRunner).

Live layers may capture FileSystem.FileSystem at construction via Layer.effect; Fake layers acquire it per-call via Layer.succeed + yield* inside the method, matching AgentRunnerEcho so fake + BunContext.layer compose with no residual R.
All absence modeled with Option<T> from effect. No T | undefined | null. All path fields use the ambient AbsolutePath brand.

New ambient types go in src/types/*.d.ts. When an import is required, wrap the file body in declare global { ... } (pattern already used in LoopRunner.d.ts and ContextEngine.d.ts). Do not add export {} to ambient files.
Tests live in sibling __tests__/ folders, named <SourceName>.test.ts. No singular __test__/ — plural per CLAUDE.md.

No any, no as unknown as. Brand-construction MUST import the existing `brand<B>(s)` helper from src/services/brand.ts — do NOT redeclare it locally in any file this feature creates or edits. Import paths relative to the consumer: from src/services/<file>.ts use `import { brand } from "./brand";`; from src/services/__tests__/<file>.ts use `import { brand } from "../brand";`; from src/services/<subdir>/<file>.ts use `import { brand } from "../brand";`; from src/services/<subdir>/__tests__/<file>.ts use `import { brand } from "../../brand";`. The `as` cast lives only inside src/services/brand.ts — this is the boundary. (Older test files at src/services/__tests__/{ContextEngine,AgentRunner,EvalParser}.test.ts predate brand.ts and still redeclare locally; do not touch them unless explicitly listed in a task's files.)
Schema uses effect's built-in Schema via `import { Schema } from "effect"` (effect@3.21.1 already pinned). Do not add @effect/schema.
FeatureContract.save uses naked fs.writeFileString. Atomic temp-and-rename is explicitly deferred to the future sandbox feature (see SPECS.md <feature:open_questions>).

gitStatus capture uses CommandExecutor.string(Command.make("git","status","--short")) in a narrow helper at src/services/LoopRunner/gitStatus.ts. Non-zero git exit returns empty string, not a tagged failure. No new Git service.

Prior-eval threading is archive-per-attempt: before retry N, copy eval/EVAL_RESULT.md to eval/archive/<taskId>/iter-{N-1:03d}-EVAL_RESULT.md, pass Option.some(archivePath) as ComposerRenderInput.priorEval. Attempt 1 passes Option.none().

Exhausted-attempts behavior is halt-with-progress-preserved: first task hitting maxAttempts is marked status=failed, contract saved, loop halts, resume hint printed. Re-run skips failed tasks (they are not schedulable) until user manually resets status + attempts or bumps maxAttempts.
Dep-cycle detection at load time in FeatureContract.load. Never enter the loop with an invalid contract.
No CLI scope. scripts/bootstrap.ts remains as the entry point after S7, shrunk to a ~15-line layer-composition shim over LoopRunner.run. A future tap run CLI feature replaces it.

Bootstrappability invariant: every task must be buildable under the current scripts/bootstrap.ts + runTask + existing agents/skills. No task may depend on LoopRunner or FeatureContract existing mid-flight. The build order and depends_on graph enforce this; S7 is last.
MAX_ITERATIONS safety cap in LoopRunner is 100. Hitting it is a bug, not a tunable.

Contract-reality escape hatch. If a mechanism-level criterion (specific API call, file path, import form, exact type construction) cannot be satisfied because it (a) produces a tsc error, (b) conflicts with another acceptance criterion in the same task, or (c) contradicts a global constraint, the Composer MUST substitute the narrowest equivalent that satisfies every behavioral criterion, and add a 1–2 sentence comment at the substitution site explaining what was substituted and why the literal criterion couldn't stand. Reviewer MUST classify such a substitution as Satisfied when the behavioral requirement is met and the comment is present. This applies ONLY to genuine contract-reality mismatches — not to Composer preference, difficulty, or stylistic disagreement.

tasks.title: Implement FeatureContract Tag + FeatureContractLive + pure helpers (nextReady, markStatus, incrementAttempt, findCycle)

acceptance.behavioral: src/services/FeatureContract.ts exports `class FeatureContract extends Context.Tag('FeatureContract')<FeatureContract, { load: (path: AbsolutePath) => Effect.Effect<Feature, FeatureContractError>; save: (path: AbsolutePath, feature: Feature) => Effect.Effect<void, FeatureContractError>; nextReady: (feature: Feature) => Option.Option<Task>; markStatus: (feature: Feature, taskId: TaskId, status: TaskStatus) => Feature; incrementAttempt: (feature: Feature, taskId: TaskId) => Feature }>() {}`
src/services/FeatureContract.ts exports FeatureContractLive: Layer.Layer<FeatureContract, never, FileSystem.FileSystem> built via Layer.effect(FeatureContract, Effect.gen(...))

load reads the file via fs.readFileString (errors → ContractReadFailed), parses JSON via Effect.try (errors → ContractInvalidJson), decodes via Schema.decodeUnknown(FeatureSchema) (errors → ContractSchemaFailed with formatted issues string), and runs findCycle (errors → ContractCycleDetected)

mechanism.value: fs.readFileString, Effect.try, Schema.decodeUnknown(FeatureSchema), findCycle, ContractReadFailed, ContractInvalidJson, ContractSchemaFailed, ContractCycleDetected

acceptance.behavioral: save calls JSON.stringify(feature, null, 2) + '\n' then fs.writeFileString; any error maps to ContractWriteFailed

nextReady is a pure function that returns the first task where (status === 'pending' || status === 'in_progress') && attempts < maxAttempts && depends_on.every(dep => doneIds.has(dep)); failed tasks are NOT schedulable and do not contribute to doneIds
markStatus and incrementAttempt are pure immutable updates that return a new Feature with the matched task patched and no other fields changed
findCycle performs DFS over the depends_on graph and on cycle returns ReadonlyArray<TaskId> naming the cycle

acceptance.behavioral: src/services/LoopRunner/LoopRunner.ts exports `class LoopRunner extends Context.Tag('LoopRunner')<LoopRunner, { run: (contractPath: AbsolutePath) => Effect.Effect<LoopSummary, FeatureContractError | RunTaskError, FeatureContract | RunTask | CommandExecutor.CommandExecutor | FileSystem.FileSystem> }>() {}` and also exports the MAX_ITERATIONS constant (= 100)

src/services/LoopRunner/LoopRunnerLive.ts exports LoopRunnerLive: Layer.Layer<LoopRunner, never, never> via Layer.succeed with FileSystem, CommandExecutor, FeatureContract, and RunTask acquired inside run via yield*

the run method follows the pipeline: load contract; loop up to MAX_ITERATIONS — nextReady (Option.none → break); compute attempt = task.attempts + 1; markStatus/incrementAttempt to in_progress; fc.save; archive prior eval if attempt > 1; captureGitStatus; rt.run wrapped in Effect.either; on Right(PASS) → markStatus done + save + continue; on Right(FAIL) or Left(error) → if attempt < maxAttempts save + continue, else markStatus failed + save + break with TaskExhausted

mechanism.value: Effect.either, fc.nextReady, fc.markStatus, fc.incrementAttempt, fc.save, archivePriorEval, captureGitStatus, rt.run

acceptance.behavioral: paths passed to RunTask.run are constructed as { featureRoot: dirname(contractPath) branded, specsPath: join(featureRoot, 'SPECS.md') branded, contractPath, attempt, priorEvalPath, gitStatus }

LoopSummary is emitted on break with feature, iterations, completed (stoppedReason._tag === 'AllDone'), stoppedReason, and tasksDone/tasksFailed/tasksPending partitioned from the flattened task list by status
when tasksFailed is non-empty, a resume-hint block is printed to stdout via Effect.sync(() => console.log(...)) matching the format in SPECS.md

acceptance.behavioral: scripts/bootstrap.ts is approximately 15–30 lines of runtime code (imports may add more lines) with no scheduling logic, no local helpers for findReadyTask, updateTask, or outcome matching

scripts/bootstrap.ts parses featureSlug = process.argv[2], constructs contractPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), '.tap/features/' + featureSlug + '/FEATURE_CONTRACT.json') branded as AbsolutePath, composes appLayer = Layer.mergeAll(LoopRunnerLive, FeatureContractLive, RunTaskLive, ContextEngineLive, EvalParserLive, AgentRunnerLive) piped with Layer.provideMerge(BunContext.layer), then yields LoopRunner, calls runner.run(contractPath), and prints the resulting LoopSummary

tasks.description: Shrink scripts/bootstrap.ts to a shim over LoopRunner.run

description: Replace the ad-hoc driver at scripts/bootstrap.ts with an Effect-based LoopRunner service plus a schema-validated FeatureContract service. Closes the five limitations listed in bootstrap.ts L11-L15 and the two RunTask.ts L129/L130 TODOs blocking judged-retry. Ships as one vertical slice: FeatureContract + RunTask-as-Tag + LoopRunner + two helpers (gitStatus, archive) + bootstrap.ts shrunk to a 15-line shim.
